title: Assigning Pods to Nodes — nominatedNodeName
text:
nominatedNodeName
can be used for external components to nominate node for a pending pod.
This nomination is best effort: it might be ignored if the scheduler determines the pod cannot go to a nominated node.
Also, this field can be (over)written by the scheduler:
If the scheduler finds a node to nominate via the preemption.
If the scheduler decides where the pod is going, and move it to the binding cycle.
Note that, in this case,
nominatedNodeName
is put only when the pod has to go through
WaitOnPermit
or
PreBind
extension points.
Note that, in this case,
nominatedNodeName
is put only when the pod has to go through
WaitOnPermit
or
PreBind
extension points.
Here is an example of a Pod status using the
nominatedNodeName
field:
apiVersion
:
v1
kind
:
Pod
metadata
:
name
:
nginx
...
status
:
nominatedNodeName
:
kube-01
